project.title: Treachery of hepatic protector triggers Notch signaling to promote hepatocyte-originated cholangiocarcinoma
project.projectDescription: Here, we found mesencephalic astrocyte-derived neurotrophic factor (MANF), a hepatic protector, was upregulated in human ICC tissues and sleeping beauty transposon (SBT) or thioacetamide (TAA)-induced mice ICC tissues. We verified the oncogenic role of MANF in ICC using cell lines overexpressing/knocked down MANF and mice specifically knocked in/out MANF in hepatocytes. Lineage tracing revealed that MANF promoted mature hepatocytes transformation into ICC. Mechanistically, we found a complex composed of MANF, CK19, and Notch2 intracellular domain (NICD2). MANF interacted with CK19 at Ser35 to suppress CK19 phosphorylation and membrane recruitment. Cytosolic CK19 bound to AR domain of NICD2 to stabilize NICD2 protein level and trigger Notch signaling, which contributed to hepatocytes transformation to ICC. We uncover a novel role of MANF and the original mechanism，which shed light on ICC intervention and treatment.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The Hucct1 cells were stimulated with TNFα(50 ng/ml) for 12 hours, and  then extracted protein with lysis buffer for Co-IP.Briefly, protein A/G magnetic beads mixed with antibody firstly, then the beads were mixed with tissues or cell lysis and then eluted. The indicated bound proteins were detected using western blot. We stained the gel after electrophoresis with silver, cut the strip into IgG control and IP groups, and sequenced it by MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: We used the search engine Mascot 2.2.2 to test the Mass spectrometry raw file and retrieve the corresponding number.According to the library, the protein result of identification was finally obtained.According to the number of peptides ,the binding fraction score, the expectvalue,and we  use uniprot, NCBI and other tools to aim the target molecules we want to study.
